methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Heirloom Carbon is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Shashank Samala, having raised $150M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of six private companies at the same stage and sector, supplemented by one public comparable (LNZA), spanning a wide range from $120M to $5.0B in valuation.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $150M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $285M. Peer-median analysis across the six same-stage, same-sector comparables—including Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M)—yields a median valuation of $355M. The peer set exhibits substantial dispersion, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B, reflecting heterogeneity in market positioning and technology maturity within the frontier climate-tech cohort.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $242M to $408M, with a median of $320M. This range reflects reasonable divergence between the stage-progression method (which anchors to historical deployment) and peer-median pricing (which captures current market multiples). Key uncertainties include the appropriate peer comparables for direct carbon removal versus alternative climate mitigation approaches, and the degree to which Commonwealth Fusion's exceptional valuation represents an outlier within the peer set.
